free_text: Cross sections for formation of Y, Sr and Rb
radionuclides induced by proton irradiation of natSr
The sample foils were prepared by a sedimentation
technique from fine Sr(NO3)2 powder deposited onto
50 um Al backing foil. The thickness of the sediment
layer was between 2.9 and 5.5 mg/cm2 Sr(NO3)2,
equivalent to natSr layers between 4.74 and 8.83 um
for the targets used in the high energy irradiation
while it was 3.55-16.54 mg/cm2 Sr(NO3)2, equivalent
to 5.66-26.33 um natSr for the lower energy stack.
Each sample was covered by a 10 ?m protection Al sheet.
Two stacks were irradiated both for 3600s, 100nA.
The adopted beam parameters were for the two stacks
Decay data were taken from NUDAT2 online data library.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of energy loss of
each foil due to thickness variation and straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primari incident beam
energy was +- 0.2 MeV which increased up to +- 1.2 MeV
in the last foil of the stack.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
